text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village nestled between verdant woods and sparkling streams lived a young girl named Laurel. Though small in stature, Laurel was known far and wide for her gentle heart and an unusual collection of antique dolls she loved.

Every evening after school, Laurel would sneak home through the whispering trees. You see, she had a secret ritual. After bath-time, she'd place each cuddly doll beside their velvet sleepsacks and listen to their silent dreams.

One peculiar doll stood out—an antique doll dressed in Colonial garb. This doll had feathers instead of hair. Laurel named it Olive.

One snowy winter night, a great storm blew through the village. Wind howled and snowflakes danced like little white owls. Lightning split the darkness.

In the midst of howling wind, Olive's feathers molted. Slowly, an intricate owl appeared—one she remembered fondly from an old children’s book. Laurel saw herself in the owl.

That night, Laurel slept with
